title: Named volume not mounted on container start
body:
/kind bug

I have a problem with named volumes where I'm not sure if it's a bug or me
doing something wrong.

When starting a container with a named volume, the content of that volume is not mounted
and therefore not visible in the container.

I created a named volume which looks like this:

```
[root@frogstar ~]# podman volume inspect FileServerData
[
    {
        "name": "FileServerData",
        "labels": {},
        "mountPoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/FileServerData/_data",
        "driver": "local",
        "options": {
            "device": "/dev/md127p1",
            "type": "xfs"
        },
        "scope": "local"
    }
]
```


Then I start a container like this:
`podman run -d -p 139:139 -p 445:445 --volume FileServerData:/shares frogstar_samba`

The container is running, but /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/FileServerData/_data
is still empty, "mount" shows that /dev/md127p1 is not mounted, as I would expect.

With docker, the same way lead to a filled /shares directory in the container,
so I wonder if more steps are needed with podman, or what else could be wrong.


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------


Related info:
[root@frogstar ~]# podman --version
podman version 1.2.0

[root@frogstar ~]# podman info --debug

comments.body:
This is a bug and we have a related problem with nfs.  
I have a PR here https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2415 that handles this for NFS, but @mheon is working this into the new rewrite of the volume handling.
Working on code for this now. Support for XFS volume may take a little longer - I'm targetting tmpfs first, then NFS/XFS/others. Hopefully all will be available in Podman 1.4
Podman inspect fixes took priority. Hopefully next sprint I'll get this in.
Relabelling as this is a new feature (support for non-directory local volumes)

Is this really implemented now? Here's what I get when I try to use it:
[root@frogstar ~]# podman --version
podman version 1.6.2
[root@frogstar ~]# podman volume create --driver local --opt type=xfs --opt device=/dev/md/jbod1 FileServerData         Error: error running volume create option: not yet implemented
[root@frogstar ~]# podman volume create --driver local --opt type=none --opt device=/mnt/data --opt o=bind FileServerData
Error: error running volume create option: not yet implemented

Both ways (using the XFS filesystem or using the already mounted filesystem's directory) don't seem to work.
Am I missing something?

The missing bit is 'driver=local' - leave that off for now, as we haven't
implemented volume plugin support yet. The command should work without it.

On Sat, Nov 16, 2019, 04:09 Branko Kannenberg <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> Is this really implemented now? Here's what I get when I try to use it:
> [root@frogstar ~]# podman --version
> podman version 1.6.2
> [root@frogstar ~]# podman volume create --driver local --opt type=xfs
> --opt device=/dev/md/jbod1 FileServerData Error: error running volume
> create option: not yet implemented
> [root@frogstar ~]# podman volume create --driver local --opt type=none
> --opt device=/mnt/data --opt o=bind FileServerData
> Error: error running volume create option: not yet implemented
>
> Both ways (using the XFS filesystem or using the already mounted
> filesystem's directory) don't seem to work.
> Am I missing something?
>
